title: gentianella lutescens subsp. lutescens
intro: Gentianella lutescens subsp. lutescens, also known as Yellow gentian or gentianella, is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family (Gentianaceae). It is a perennial herb with yellow flowers that grows in the mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. The medicinal properties of gentianella include its bitter compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and stimulate appetite. The health benefits of gentianella include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve nausea, and support the liver and gallbladder function, and it can be prepared as a tea or infused in water for herbal consumption.
